mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate over the recent month signals easier discounting conditions and a lower equity risk premium, so S&P 500 returns should be positive over the next 20 trading days when policy is moving down.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in RSAFS above its recent trend indicates stronger consumer spending and revenue momentum, which should lift S&P 500 prices over the next month as earnings expectations improve.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises above its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not beat unconditional 20-day returns by at least 1 percentage point on average, or if positive return frequency falls below 50% across triggered samples.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises quickly, it signals weakening household income and deteriorating cyclical demand, so S&P 500 forward returns should be negative over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered windows do not produce lower 20-day returns than untriggered windows, or if the sign is positive in more than 45% of trigger events.

mechanism_narrative: Improving INDPRO together with rising PAYEMS confirms broad real activity expansion, and the joint signal should forecast positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through earnings and sentiment channels.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined-signal portfolio does not outperform either single-series signal over 60-day horizons, or if joint-upturn episodes fail to show positive average returns.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of elevated FEDFUNDS and accelerating CPIAUCSL implies policy is tightening behind inflation, raising real discount rates and compressing equity valuations, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation is rising
failure_condition: Reject if joint high-rate/high-inflation triggers do not produce negative average 20-day returns, or if the interaction term adds no explanatory power versus FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST below its recent trend signals weakening housing demand and broader cyclical softness, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if below-trend housing episodes do not underperform baseline 20-day returns, or if the signal remains positive in more than half of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A rising PAYEMS series during a fragile labor market implies payroll stabilization and supports consumption income, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as risk appetite improves.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE is flat or falling while PAYEMS rises month over month
failure_condition: Reject if the combined payroll-unemployment stabilization signal fails to generate positive 5-day returns on average, or if the effect disappears when UNRATE is not falling.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, simultaneous strengthening of GDPC1 and RSAFS indicates that both final demand and realized sales are recovering, so S&P 500 returns should be positive over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint GDP-sales recoveries do not outperform baseline 60-day returns, or if the signal is not materially stronger than either proxy alone in walk-forward tests.
